model_narrative: We plan the world aluminum industry over a single investment horizon. Bauxite is mined in a number of mining regions, shipped to producing regions where it is refined into alumina, and the alumina is then smelted with electricity into aluminum and shipped to marketing areas to meet demand. We decide how much of each bauxite grade to mine in each region, how much of each refining and smelting process to run in each producing region, how much electricity of each supply type to draw, and the full set of shipments of bauxite, alumina, and finished aluminum across regions and markets. We also decide how much new mine, refinery, and smelter capacity to build, choosing discrete investment segments whose selection is governed by build indicators. The objective is to minimize the total cost, which gathers mine and refinery operating costs, electricity, transport, and the annualized cost of all new capacity investments.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: mining regions where bauxite is extracted

sets.name: r
sets.doc: producing regions that host refineries and smelters; alumina and aluminum are made here

sets.name: j
sets.doc: marketing areas where final aluminum is demanded

sets.name: c
sets.doc: all commodities: the various bauxite grades, the alumina intermediate, final aluminum, and electricity

sets.name: ci

sets.name: cf

sets.name: cl
sets.doc: the electricity commodity, a subset of the commodities consumed by smelting

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the refining and smelting processes; each refining process turns one bauxite grade into alumina, and the smelting process turns alumina plus electricity into aluminum

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the productive units (physical plants): refineries and smelters

sets.name: mr
sets.doc: the refining units, a subset of the productive units

sets.name: ms
sets.doc: the smelting unit, a subset of the productive units

sets.name: seg
sets.doc: the discrete investment size segments used to build out new capacity in fixed increments

sets.name: l
sets.doc: the electricity supply types (price tiers); el-hicost is an unlimited high-cost backstop, the others have finite availability

sets.name: f
sets.doc: trade blocs used to write inter-bloc trade restrictions

sets.name: cpospi
sets.doc: the set of feasible (mining region, bauxite grade) pairs; a mine can only produce the bauxite grades paired with it here, and any quantity for an unlisted pair is meaningless

sets.name: fr

sets.name: fi

sets.name: fj

params.name: interval
params.doc: the number of years in the planning interval, used to convert an annual mine output rate into a cumulative extraction over the interval for the reserve check

params.name: utm
params.doc: the fraction of installed mine capacity that is actually usable (capacity utilization for mines)

params.name: sigma
params.doc: the capital recovery factor that annualizes lump-sum investment outlays

params.name: gamma
params.doc: the fraction of a region's trade flow that is exempt from the bloc self-sufficiency rule; the complement, one minus gamma, is the share that must be met internally

params.name: pl
params.doc: the world market price of aluminum

params.name: a
params.doc: input-output coefficients per unit of process activity, in tons of commodity per unit of process; negative values denote inputs consumed and positive values denote outputs produced

params.name: b
params.doc: the amount of a productive unit's capacity used per unit of process activity

params.name: d
params.doc: the aluminum demand in each marketing area

params.name: nmaa2000
params.doc: non-metallurgical alumina demand drawn off in each producing region

params.name: nmba2000
params.doc: non-metallurgical bauxite demand drawn off in each mining region

params.name: zmbar
params.doc: the maximum cumulative bauxite output level available from each mining region over the planning interval (the reserve ceiling)

params.name: capm
params.doc: the existing plus already-committed mine capacity in each mining region

params.name: capr
params.doc: the existing plus already-committed refinery or smelter capacity for each productive unit in each producing region

params.name: utr
params.doc: the fraction of installed refinery or smelter capacity that is actually usable for each productive unit

params.name: sbm
params.doc: the mine plant-size increment associated with each investment segment in each mining region

params.name: sbr
params.doc: the refinery or smelter plant-size increment associated with each investment segment, per unit and producing region

params.name: om
params.doc: the operating cost per unit of bauxite mined in each mining region

params.name: ors
params.doc: the operating cost per unit of process activity at refineries and smelters in each producing region

params.name: muf
params.doc: the unit transport cost for shipping final aluminum from a producing region to a marketing area

params.name: mui
params.doc: the unit transport cost for shipping alumina between producing regions (interplant)

params.name: mur
params.doc: the unit transport cost for shipping bauxite from a mining region to a producing region

params.name: omegam
params.doc: the fixed lump-sum investment cost of a mine expansion at each segment in each mining region

params.name: omegar
params.doc: the fixed lump-sum investment cost of a refinery or smelter expansion at each segment, per unit and producing region

params.name: prelec
params.doc: the electricity price by supply type in each producing region; a zero entry marks a supply type that is unavailable in that region

params.name: ubar
params.doc: the upper bound on electricity availability by supply type in each producing region

vars.doc: the quantity of final aluminum shipped from a producing region to a marketing area

vars.name: xi
vars.doc: the quantity of alumina shipped between producing regions; the first index is the origin and the second the destination

vars.name: xm
vars.doc: the quantity of a bauxite grade shipped from a mining region to a producing region

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the activity level of each refining or smelting process run in each producing region

vars.name: zm
vars.doc: the output level of each bauxite grade mined in each mining region

vars.name: hm
vars.doc: the continuous amount of new mine capacity added in each mining region

vars.name: hr
vars.doc: the continuous amount of new refinery or smelter capacity added for each unit in each producing region

vars.name: sm
vars.doc: the selection weight on each mine investment segment in each mining region; a convex-combination weight that picks how much of a segment's size increment is taken

vars.name: sr
vars.doc: the selection weight on each refinery or smelter investment segment, per unit and producing region

vars.name: ym
vars.doc: the build indicator for a mine expansion in each mining region; one if any expansion is undertaken there and zero otherwise

vars.name: yr
vars.doc: the build indicator for a refinery or smelter expansion for each unit in each producing region; one if any expansion is undertaken and zero otherwise

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the electricity supplied of each type in each producing region

vars.name: phiom
vars.doc: the total mine operating cost (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phior
vars.doc: the total refinery and smelter operating cost including electricity (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phit
vars.doc: the total transport cost (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phikm
vars.doc: the total annualized mine investment cost (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phikr
vars.doc: the total annualized refinery and smelter investment cost (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phi4
vars.doc: the grand-total cost (an accounting variable)

constraints.name: mbm
constraints.intent: For each mining region and each bauxite grade it can actually produce, the amount of that grade mined there must be enough to cover everything shipped from that region to the producing regions plus the non-metallurgical bauxite demand drawn off locally. Grades a region cannot produce are not constrained.

constraints.name: mbr
constraints.intent: In every producing region each commodity must balance, with the supply at least covering the use. Supply comes from whatever the local processes produce of that commodity, plus, for a bauxite grade, the inflows of that grade shipped in from the mining regions that can make it; for alumina, the inflows shipped in from other producing regions; and for electricity, the electricity supplied locally by the available supply types. The requirements that must be met are, for final aluminum, everything shipped out to the marketing areas, and for alumina, everything shipped out to other producing regions plus the local non-metallurgical alumina demand. Processes consume their inputs, which already enters supply as a negative production amount.

constraints.name: fdb
constraints.intent: Each marketing area must receive at least its aluminum demand. For every marketing area the total final aluminum shipped in from all producing regions must be at least the demand there.

constraints.name: res
constraints.intent: Mining cannot exhaust a region's reserves over the planning horizon. For each mining region and each bauxite grade it can produce, the annual output level scaled up over the length of the planning interval must not exceed that region's available reserve. Grades a region cannot produce are not constrained.

constraints.name: ccm
constraints.intent: Mining in a region is limited by its usable mine capacity. For each mining region, the total output summed over the bauxite grades it can produce must not exceed the usable fraction of its capacity, where capacity is the existing committed capacity plus any new capacity built there.

constraints.name: ccr
constraints.intent: Each refinery or smelter unit in a producing region is limited by its usable capacity. For every unit and producing region, the capacity that the processes draw on that unit must not exceed the usable fraction of its capacity, where capacity is the existing committed capacity plus any new capacity built for that unit there.

constraints.name: i1m
constraints.intent: The new mine capacity added in a region is defined by the discrete investment segments chosen. For each mining region, the amount of new mine capacity must equal the sum over the investment segments of each segment's size increment weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: i1r
constraints.intent: The new refinery or smelter capacity added is defined by the discrete investment segments chosen. For every unit and producing region, the amount of new capacity must equal the sum over the investment segments of each segment's size increment weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: i2m
constraints.intent: A mine expansion is marked as built only to the extent that investment segments are selected. For each mining region, the build indicator must equal the total selection weight summed over all investment segments, which ties undertaking any expansion to the indicator.

constraints.name: i2r
constraints.intent: A refinery or smelter expansion is marked as built only to the extent that investment segments are selected. For every unit and producing region, the build indicator must equal the total selection weight summed over all investment segments.

constraints.name: tba
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, bauxite trade must be self-sufficient apart from an exempt share. Looking at all bauxite shipments into producing regions of the bloc, the shipments that originate from mining regions inside the same bloc must be at least the exempt fraction of all such shipments into the bloc. Only feasible mine-and-grade combinations enter this accounting.

constraints.name: taa
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, alumina supply must be largely internal. The bloc's own alumina production, reduced to the non-exempt share, must be at least the alumina shipped into the bloc's producing regions from regions outside the bloc. A region's alumina production is what its smelting activity consumes as input.

constraints.name: tal
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, finished aluminum trade must be self-sufficient apart from an exempt share. Looking at all aluminum shipments into the marketing areas of the bloc, the shipments that originate from producing regions inside the same bloc must be at least the exempt fraction of all such shipments into the bloc.

constraints.name: aom
constraints.intent: Total mine operating cost accumulates the cost of everything mined. Set the mine operating cost accounting variable equal to, over every feasible mining region and bauxite grade, the per-unit mining cost times the amount mined, expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: aor
constraints.intent: Total refinery and smelter operating cost gathers process running costs and electricity. Set the refinery and smelter operating cost accounting variable equal to the sum over producing regions and processes of the per-unit process cost times its activity, plus the sum over producing regions and electricity supply types of the electricity price times the electricity supplied, expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: at
constraints.intent: Total transport cost gathers all shipping. Set the transport cost accounting variable equal to, summed over producing regions, the cost of final aluminum shipments to the marketing areas, plus the cost of alumina shipments to other producing regions, plus the cost of bauxite shipments from feasible mining regions, each valued at its unit transport cost, all expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: akm
constraints.intent: Total mine investment cost annualizes the fixed cost of the mine expansions chosen. Set the mine investment cost accounting variable equal to the capital recovery factor times the sum over investment segments and mining regions of each segment's fixed expansion cost weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: akr
constraints.intent: Total refinery and smelter investment cost annualizes the fixed cost of the expansions chosen. Set the refinery and smelter investment cost accounting variable equal to the capital recovery factor times the sum over investment segments, producing regions, and units of each segment's fixed expansion cost weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: a4
constraints.intent: The grand-total cost adds up all the cost components. Set the total cost accounting variable equal to the sum of the transport cost, the mine operating cost, the refinery and smelter operating cost, the mine investment cost, and the refinery and smelter investment cost.

query: Washington is floating a North American supply-security target: the Western US plants should be covering at least 40% of all the metal delivered into the Western North America market. What total cost does the optimizer return if we hold to that?
expected_pyomo: model.na_local_floor = Constraint(expr=model.xf['western-us', 'wn-america'] >= 0.4 * sum(model.xf[r, 'wn-america'] for r in model.r))

constraints.name: mbm
constraints.intent: For each mining region and each bauxite grade it can actually produce, the amount of that grade mined there must be enough to cover everything shipped from that region to the producing regions plus the non-metallurgical bauxite demand drawn off locally. Grades a region cannot produce are not constrained.

constraints.name: mbr
constraints.intent: In every producing region each commodity must balance, with the supply at least covering the use. Supply comes from whatever the local processes produce of that commodity, plus, for a bauxite grade, the inflows of that grade shipped in from the mining regions that can make it; for alumina, the inflows shipped in from other producing regions; and for electricity, the electricity supplied locally by the available supply types. The requirements that must be met are, for final aluminum, everything shipped out to the marketing areas, and for alumina, everything shipped out to other producing regions plus the local non-metallurgical alumina demand. Processes consume their inputs, which already enters supply as a negative production amount.

constraints.name: fdb
constraints.intent: Each marketing area must receive at least its aluminum demand. For every marketing area the total final aluminum shipped in from all producing regions must be at least the demand there.

constraints.name: res
constraints.intent: Mining cannot exhaust a region's reserves over the planning horizon. For each mining region and each bauxite grade it can produce, the annual output level scaled up over the length of the planning interval must not exceed that region's available reserve. Grades a region cannot produce are not constrained.

constraints.name: ccm
constraints.intent: Mining in a region is limited by its usable mine capacity. For each mining region, the total output summed over the bauxite grades it can produce must not exceed the usable fraction of its capacity, where capacity is the existing committed capacity plus any new capacity built there.

constraints.name: ccr
constraints.intent: Each refinery or smelter unit in a producing region is limited by its usable capacity. For every unit and producing region, the capacity that the processes draw on that unit must not exceed the usable fraction of its capacity, where capacity is the existing committed capacity plus any new capacity built for that unit there.

constraints.name: i1m
constraints.intent: The new mine capacity added in a region is defined by the discrete investment segments chosen. For each mining region, the amount of new mine capacity must equal the sum over the investment segments of each segment's size increment weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: i1r
constraints.intent: The new refinery or smelter capacity added is defined by the discrete investment segments chosen. For every unit and producing region, the amount of new capacity must equal the sum over the investment segments of each segment's size increment weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: i2m
constraints.intent: A mine expansion is marked as built only to the extent that investment segments are selected. For each mining region, the build indicator must equal the total selection weight summed over all investment segments, which ties undertaking any expansion to the indicator.

constraints.name: i2r
constraints.intent: A refinery or smelter expansion is marked as built only to the extent that investment segments are selected. For every unit and producing region, the build indicator must equal the total selection weight summed over all investment segments.

constraints.name: tba
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, bauxite trade must be self-sufficient apart from an exempt share. Looking at all bauxite shipments into producing regions of the bloc, the shipments that originate from mining regions inside the same bloc must be at least the exempt fraction of all such shipments into the bloc. Only feasible mine-and-grade combinations enter this accounting.

constraints.name: taa
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, alumina supply must be largely internal. The bloc's own alumina production, reduced to the non-exempt share, must be at least the alumina shipped into the bloc's producing regions from regions outside the bloc. A region's alumina production is what its smelting activity consumes as input.

constraints.name: tal
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, finished aluminum trade must be self-sufficient apart from an exempt share. Looking at all aluminum shipments into the marketing areas of the bloc, the shipments that originate from producing regions inside the same bloc must be at least the exempt fraction of all such shipments into the bloc.

constraints.name: aom
constraints.intent: Total mine operating cost accumulates the cost of everything mined. Set the mine operating cost accounting variable equal to, over every feasible mining region and bauxite grade, the per-unit mining cost times the amount mined, expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: aor
constraints.intent: Total refinery and smelter operating cost gathers process running costs and electricity. Set the refinery and smelter operating cost accounting variable equal to the sum over producing regions and processes of the per-unit process cost times its activity, plus the sum over producing regions and electricity supply types of the electricity price times the electricity supplied, expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: at
constraints.intent: Total transport cost gathers all shipping. Set the transport cost accounting variable equal to, summed over producing regions, the cost of final aluminum shipments to the marketing areas, plus the cost of alumina shipments to other producing regions, plus the cost of bauxite shipments from feasible mining regions, each valued at its unit transport cost, all expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: akm
constraints.intent: Total mine investment cost annualizes the fixed cost of the mine expansions chosen. Set the mine investment cost accounting variable equal to the capital recovery factor times the sum over investment segments and mining regions of each segment's fixed expansion cost weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: akr
constraints.intent: Total refinery and smelter investment cost annualizes the fixed cost of the expansions chosen. Set the refinery and smelter investment cost accounting variable equal to the capital recovery factor times the sum over investment segments, producing regions, and units of each segment's fixed expansion cost weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: a4
constraints.intent: The grand-total cost adds up all the cost components. Set the total cost accounting variable equal to the sum of the transport cost, the mine operating cost, the refinery and smelter operating cost, the mine investment cost, and the refinery and smelter investment cost.

query: Washington is negotiating a package deal with us, and both clauses land together: the Western US caps how much it pushes into the Western North America market at 1500 tonnes, and in exchange the Eastern US commits to covering at least 55% of that same market. Run the two together — where does total cost land?
expected_pyomo:
model.we_self_cap = Constraint(expr=model.xf['western-us', 'wn-america'] <= 1500)
model.wus_we_floor = Constraint(expr=model.xf['eastern-us', 'wn-america'] >= 0.55 * sum(model.xf[r, 'wn-america'] for r in model.r))

model_narrative: We plan the world aluminum industry over a single investment horizon. Bauxite is mined in a number of mining regions, shipped to producing regions where it is refined into alumina, and the alumina is then smelted with electricity into aluminum and shipped to marketing areas to meet demand. We decide how much of each bauxite grade to mine in each region, how much of each refining and smelting process to run in each producing region, how much electricity of each supply type to draw, and the full set of shipments of bauxite, alumina, and finished aluminum across regions and markets. We also decide how much new mine, refinery, and smelter capacity to build, choosing discrete investment segments whose selection is governed by build indicators. The objective is to minimize the total cost, which gathers mine and refinery operating costs, electricity, transport, and the annualized cost of all new capacity investments.

query: The Central America and Caribbean market leans heavily on Guyana — one strike or one dry hydro reservoir there and the whole region is stranded. Shouldn't Guyana be held to at most 40% of the combined deliveries all producing regions make into Central America and the Caribbean? What's the model seeing that justifies leaning so hard on a single source?
expected_pyomo: model.esa_split = Constraint(expr=model.xf['guyana', 'c-amer+car'] <= 0.4 * sum(model.xf[r, 'c-amer+car'] for r in model.r))

constraints.name: mbm
constraints.intent: For each mining region and each bauxite grade it can actually produce, the amount of that grade mined there must be enough to cover everything shipped from that region to the producing regions plus the non-metallurgical bauxite demand drawn off locally. Grades a region cannot produce are not constrained.

constraints.name: mbr
constraints.intent: In every producing region each commodity must balance, with the supply at least covering the use. Supply comes from whatever the local processes produce of that commodity, plus, for a bauxite grade, the inflows of that grade shipped in from the mining regions that can make it; for alumina, the inflows shipped in from other producing regions; and for electricity, the electricity supplied locally by the available supply types. The requirements that must be met are, for final aluminum, everything shipped out to the marketing areas, and for alumina, everything shipped out to other producing regions plus the local non-metallurgical alumina demand. Processes consume their inputs, which already enters supply as a negative production amount.

constraints.name: fdb
constraints.intent: Each marketing area must receive at least its aluminum demand. For every marketing area the total final aluminum shipped in from all producing regions must be at least the demand there.

constraints.name: res
constraints.intent: Mining cannot exhaust a region's reserves over the planning horizon. For each mining region and each bauxite grade it can produce, the annual output level scaled up over the length of the planning interval must not exceed that region's available reserve. Grades a region cannot produce are not constrained.

constraints.name: ccm
constraints.intent: Mining in a region is limited by its usable mine capacity. For each mining region, the total output summed over the bauxite grades it can produce must not exceed the usable fraction of its capacity, where capacity is the existing committed capacity plus any new capacity built there.

constraints.name: ccr
constraints.intent: Each refinery or smelter unit in a producing region is limited by its usable capacity. For every unit and producing region, the capacity that the processes draw on that unit must not exceed the usable fraction of its capacity, where capacity is the existing committed capacity plus any new capacity built for that unit there.

constraints.name: i1m
constraints.intent: The new mine capacity added in a region is defined by the discrete investment segments chosen. For each mining region, the amount of new mine capacity must equal the sum over the investment segments of each segment's size increment weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: i1r
constraints.intent: The new refinery or smelter capacity added is defined by the discrete investment segments chosen. For every unit and producing region, the amount of new capacity must equal the sum over the investment segments of each segment's size increment weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: i2m
constraints.intent: A mine expansion is marked as built only to the extent that investment segments are selected. For each mining region, the build indicator must equal the total selection weight summed over all investment segments, which ties undertaking any expansion to the indicator.

constraints.name: i2r
constraints.intent: A refinery or smelter expansion is marked as built only to the extent that investment segments are selected. For every unit and producing region, the build indicator must equal the total selection weight summed over all investment segments.

constraints.name: tba
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, bauxite trade must be self-sufficient apart from an exempt share. Looking at all bauxite shipments into producing regions of the bloc, the shipments that originate from mining regions inside the same bloc must be at least the exempt fraction of all such shipments into the bloc. Only feasible mine-and-grade combinations enter this accounting.

constraints.name: taa
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, alumina supply must be largely internal. The bloc's own alumina production, reduced to the non-exempt share, must be at least the alumina shipped into the bloc's producing regions from regions outside the bloc. A region's alumina production is what its smelting activity consumes as input.

constraints.name: tal
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, finished aluminum trade must be self-sufficient apart from an exempt share. Looking at all aluminum shipments into the marketing areas of the bloc, the shipments that originate from producing regions inside the same bloc must be at least the exempt fraction of all such shipments into the bloc.

constraints.name: aom
constraints.intent: Total mine operating cost accumulates the cost of everything mined. Set the mine operating cost accounting variable equal to, over every feasible mining region and bauxite grade, the per-unit mining cost times the amount mined, expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: aor
constraints.intent: Total refinery and smelter operating cost gathers process running costs and electricity. Set the refinery and smelter operating cost accounting variable equal to the sum over producing regions and processes of the per-unit process cost times its activity, plus the sum over producing regions and electricity supply types of the electricity price times the electricity supplied, expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: at
constraints.intent: Total transport cost gathers all shipping. Set the transport cost accounting variable equal to, summed over producing regions, the cost of final aluminum shipments to the marketing areas, plus the cost of alumina shipments to other producing regions, plus the cost of bauxite shipments from feasible mining regions, each valued at its unit transport cost, all expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: akm
constraints.intent: Total mine investment cost annualizes the fixed cost of the mine expansions chosen. Set the mine investment cost accounting variable equal to the capital recovery factor times the sum over investment segments and mining regions of each segment's fixed expansion cost weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: akr
constraints.intent: Total refinery and smelter investment cost annualizes the fixed cost of the expansions chosen. Set the refinery and smelter investment cost accounting variable equal to the capital recovery factor times the sum over investment segments, producing regions, and units of each segment's fixed expansion cost weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: a4
constraints.intent: The grand-total cost adds up all the cost components. Set the total cost accounting variable equal to the sum of the transport cost, the mine operating cost, the refinery and smelter operating cost, the mine investment cost, and the refinery and smelter investment cost.

query: We could end up writing fixed-expansion commitments to both the USA and the West Europe mine complexes in the same plan. Couldn't we hold it to at most one of those two sites and put the other commitment to work somewhere else? What's the tradeoff that would make the optimizer want to fund both?

model_narrative: We plan the world aluminum industry over a single investment horizon. Bauxite is mined in a number of mining regions, shipped to producing regions where it is refined into alumina, and the alumina is then smelted with electricity into aluminum and shipped to marketing areas to meet demand. We decide how much of each bauxite grade to mine in each region, how much of each refining and smelting process to run in each producing region, how much electricity of each supply type to draw, and the full set of shipments of bauxite, alumina, and finished aluminum across regions and markets. We also decide how much new mine, refinery, and smelter capacity to build, choosing discrete investment segments whose selection is governed by build indicators. The objective is to minimize the total cost, which gathers mine and refinery operating costs, electricity, transport, and the annualized cost of all new capacity investments.

query: The Central America and Caribbean market gets a chunk of its metal hauled in from farther afield, yet the Western US and Guyana sit right on its doorstep. Shouldn't those two nearby producers be supplying at least 60% of that market's demand between them? What's keeping the model from sourcing closer to home?

vars.doc: the total refinery and smelter operating cost including electricity (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phit
vars.doc: the total transport cost (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phikm
vars.doc: the total annualized mine investment cost (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phikr
vars.doc: the total annualized refinery and smelter investment cost (an accounting variable)

vars.name: phi4
vars.doc: the grand-total cost (an accounting variable)

constraints.name: mbm
constraints.intent: For each mining region and each bauxite grade it can actually produce, the amount of that grade mined there must be enough to cover everything shipped from that region to the producing regions plus the non-metallurgical bauxite demand drawn off locally. Grades a region cannot produce are not constrained.

constraints.name: mbr
constraints.intent: In every producing region each commodity must balance, with the supply at least covering the use. Supply comes from whatever the local processes produce of that commodity, plus, for a bauxite grade, the inflows of that grade shipped in from the mining regions that can make it; for alumina, the inflows shipped in from other producing regions; and for electricity, the electricity supplied locally by the available supply types. The requirements that must be met are, for final aluminum, everything shipped out to the marketing areas, and for alumina, everything shipped out to other producing regions plus the local non-metallurgical alumina demand. Processes consume their inputs, which already enters supply as a negative production amount.

constraints.name: fdb
constraints.intent: Each marketing area must receive at least its aluminum demand. For every marketing area the total final aluminum shipped in from all producing regions must be at least the demand there.

constraints.name: res
constraints.intent: Mining cannot exhaust a region's reserves over the planning horizon. For each mining region and each bauxite grade it can produce, the annual output level scaled up over the length of the planning interval must not exceed that region's available reserve. Grades a region cannot produce are not constrained.

constraints.name: ccm
constraints.intent: Mining in a region is limited by its usable mine capacity. For each mining region, the total output summed over the bauxite grades it can produce must not exceed the usable fraction of its capacity, where capacity is the existing committed capacity plus any new capacity built there.

constraints.name: ccr
constraints.intent: Each refinery or smelter unit in a producing region is limited by its usable capacity. For every unit and producing region, the capacity that the processes draw on that unit must not exceed the usable fraction of its capacity, where capacity is the existing committed capacity plus any new capacity built for that unit there.

constraints.name: i1m
constraints.intent: The new mine capacity added in a region is defined by the discrete investment segments chosen. For each mining region, the amount of new mine capacity must equal the sum over the investment segments of each segment's size increment weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: i1r
constraints.intent: The new refinery or smelter capacity added is defined by the discrete investment segments chosen. For every unit and producing region, the amount of new capacity must equal the sum over the investment segments of each segment's size increment weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: i2m
constraints.intent: A mine expansion is marked as built only to the extent that investment segments are selected. For each mining region, the build indicator must equal the total selection weight summed over all investment segments, which ties undertaking any expansion to the indicator.

constraints.name: i2r
constraints.intent: A refinery or smelter expansion is marked as built only to the extent that investment segments are selected. For every unit and producing region, the build indicator must equal the total selection weight summed over all investment segments.

constraints.name: tba
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, bauxite trade must be self-sufficient apart from an exempt share. Looking at all bauxite shipments into producing regions of the bloc, the shipments that originate from mining regions inside the same bloc must be at least the exempt fraction of all such shipments into the bloc. Only feasible mine-and-grade combinations enter this accounting.

constraints.name: taa
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, alumina supply must be largely internal. The bloc's own alumina production, reduced to the non-exempt share, must be at least the alumina shipped into the bloc's producing regions from regions outside the bloc. A region's alumina production is what its smelting activity consumes as input.

constraints.name: tal
constraints.intent: For each trade bloc, finished aluminum trade must be self-sufficient apart from an exempt share. Looking at all aluminum shipments into the marketing areas of the bloc, the shipments that originate from producing regions inside the same bloc must be at least the exempt fraction of all such shipments into the bloc.

constraints.name: aom
constraints.intent: Total mine operating cost accumulates the cost of everything mined. Set the mine operating cost accounting variable equal to, over every feasible mining region and bauxite grade, the per-unit mining cost times the amount mined, expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: aor
constraints.intent: Total refinery and smelter operating cost gathers process running costs and electricity. Set the refinery and smelter operating cost accounting variable equal to the sum over producing regions and processes of the per-unit process cost times its activity, plus the sum over producing regions and electricity supply types of the electricity price times the electricity supplied, expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: at
constraints.intent: Total transport cost gathers all shipping. Set the transport cost accounting variable equal to, summed over producing regions, the cost of final aluminum shipments to the marketing areas, plus the cost of alumina shipments to other producing regions, plus the cost of bauxite shipments from feasible mining regions, each valued at its unit transport cost, all expressed in thousands.

constraints.name: akm
constraints.intent: Total mine investment cost annualizes the fixed cost of the mine expansions chosen. Set the mine investment cost accounting variable equal to the capital recovery factor times the sum over investment segments and mining regions of each segment's fixed expansion cost weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: akr
constraints.intent: Total refinery and smelter investment cost annualizes the fixed cost of the expansions chosen. Set the refinery and smelter investment cost accounting variable equal to the capital recovery factor times the sum over investment segments, producing regions, and units of each segment's fixed expansion cost weighted by how much of that segment is selected.

constraints.name: a4
constraints.intent: The grand-total cost adds up all the cost components. Set the total cost accounting variable equal to the sum of the transport cost, the mine operating cost, the refinery and smelter operating cost, the mine investment cost, and the refinery and smelter investment cost.

query: The Eastern US sits right next to the Central America and Caribbean market, yet the plan has it putting next to nothing in while farther-off suppliers carry the load — for a backyard market that looks backwards. Shouldn't Eastern US deliveries into that market at least match everything the other regions ship there combined? What's pulling the optimizer toward the long hauls?
expected_pyomo: model.japan_home = Constraint(expr=model.xf['eastern-us', 'c-amer+car'] >= sum(model.xf[r, 'c-amer+car'] for r in model.r if r != 'eastern-us'))
